title: Uber plans Tokyo robotaxi trial with Nissan and Britain's Wayve
description: TOKYO — Uber announced Thursday its first robotaxi partnership in Japan, teaming up with Nissan and British artificial intelligence autonomous driving startup Wayve for a trial this year in Tokyo.
content:

title: Anthropic launches institute to study AI’s societal risks, says next two years crucial
description: Anthropic has launched a new institute to study the societal risks and challenges posed by rapidly advancing artificial intelligence, warning that the next two years could bring major leaps in AI capability and raise urgent questions about jobs, governance and economic impact.
content: AI is advancing with time, along with time the speed of progress is evolving but is also raising questions about how societies will manage the technology’s long-term impact. Following this debate, Anthropic has announced the launch of a new institute... [2361 chars]

title: Memory chip shortage driven by rising AI demand, prices likely to remain elevated: IESA President
description: The global shortage of memory chips is largely being driven by the rapid rise in artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, which has significantly increased demand for advanced memory solutions, according to Ashok Chandak, President of the India Electronics and Semiconductor Association (IESA) and SEMI India.

title: Five ChatGPT questions cost 1/2 litre water: Priyank Kharge bats for green data centres
description: Karnataka Minister Priyank Kharge said 32 private data centres are currently operational in Karnataka, describing them as a “necessary evil” crucial for emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and machine learning.

title: Atlassian layoffs: Company plans to cut around 1,600 jobs amid AI push
description: Atlassian plans to cut about 1,600 jobs worldwide as it restructures around artificial intelligence, highlighting how tech firms are reshaping workforces amid slowing demand and growing debate over AI-driven layoffs.

title: Atlassian to cut roughly 10% jobs in pivot to AI, Rajeev Rajan will step down as CTO
